scenarios.title: Calculate and round aggregated market-risk-component margin
scenarios.intent: Verify the correct calculation and rounding of aggregated market-risk-component margin following the two-step process
given: Rounding parameter in Initial Margin Risk Parameter File is 10,000
when: the clearing participant calculates the aggregated market-risk-component margin
the system applies rounding up to the nearest 10,000
then: the aggregated margin before rounding is calculated as 46,929,904
the rounded aggregated market-risk-component margin is 46,930,000
assumptions: All margin component values are provided in the correct currency and format
The rounding parameter is correctly configured in the Initial Margin Risk Parameter File

evidence.quote: Aggregated market-risk-component margin = Portfolio margin + Flat rate margin + Liquidation risk add-on + Structured product add-on + Corporate action position margin + Holiday add-on
Round up the aggregated margin with reference to the rounding parameter stated in the Initial Margin Risk Parameter File
46,929,904 is to be rounded up to the nearest 10,000

scenarios.title: Rounding when aggregated margin is exact multiple of rounding parameter
scenarios.intent: Verify rounding behavior when the aggregated margin is already an exact multiple of the rounding parameter
given: Aggregated margin before rounding is exactly 46,930,000
Rounding parameter in Initial Margin Risk Parameter File is 10,000
when: the system applies rounding up to the nearest 10,000
then: the rounded aggregated market-risk-component margin remains 46,930,000
no upward adjustment is applied since value is already at the rounding boundary
assumptions: When the value is already at a rounding boundary, rounding up does not increase the value
The rounding parameter of 10,000 is used as the boundary value from the evidence example

evidence.quote: Round up the aggregated margin with reference to the rounding parameter stated in the Initial Margin Risk Parameter File (e.g., 10,000)

scenarios.title: Validate all margin components are present for aggregation calculation
scenarios.intent: Verify that all required margin components are present and valid before performing the aggregation calculation
given: A clearing participant portfolio with margin calculation request
when: the system validates the presence of all margin components
the system validates the rounding parameter from the Initial Margin Risk Parameter File
then: Portfolio margin is present and valid
Flat rate margin is present and valid
Liquidation risk add-on is present and valid
Structured product add-on is present and valid
Corporate action position margin is present and valid
Holiday add-on is present and valid
Rounding parameter is retrieved from the Initial Margin Risk Parameter File
assumptions: Each margin component must be a non-negative numeric value
The rounding parameter must be a positive integer

evidence.quote: Aggregated market-risk-component margin = Portfolio margin + Flat rate margin + Liquidation risk add-on + Structured product add-on + Corporate action position margin + Holiday add-on
rounding parameter stated in the Initial Margin Risk Parameter File

scenarios.title: Apply margin credit to calculate net margin after credit
scenarios.intent: Verify the correct application of margin credit to calculate net margin after credit using the Maximum formula
when: the system calculates net margin after credit
the system applies the Maximum[0, (Net margin – Margin credit)] formula
then: net margin after credit is calculated as 41,930,000
assumptions: The margin credit value of 5,000,000 is the standard value as stated in the guide
Net margin before credit is the rounded aggregated market-risk-component margin

evidence.quote: A margin credit (normally 5,000,000) is granted to each CP
Net margin after credit = Maximum [0 , (Net margin – Margin credit)]

scenarios.title: Net margin after credit when net margin equals margin credit
scenarios.intent: Verify the boundary condition where net margin equals margin credit, resulting in zero net margin after credit
given: Net margin before credit is exactly 5,000,000
when: the system calculates net margin after credit
the system applies the Maximum[0, (Net margin – Margin credit)] formula
then: the Maximum function ensures the result is not negative
assumptions: The boundary value of 5,000,000 is derived from the margin credit value stated in the evidence
When Net margin equals Margin credit, the result is exactly 0

evidence.quote: A margin credit (normally 5,000,000) is granted to each CP
Net margin after credit = Maximum [0 , (Net margin – Margin credit)]

scenarios.title: Validate margin credit value and Maximum function application
scenarios.intent: Verify that the margin credit value is correctly configured and the Maximum function prevents negative net margin
given: A clearing participant with margin calculation request
when: the system validates the margin credit value
the system validates the Maximum function logic for net margin calculation
then: margin credit is a valid positive numeric value
the Maximum[0, (Net margin – Margin credit)] formula is correctly implemented
net margin after credit is never negative regardless of input values
assumptions: The margin credit value should match the configured value (normally 5,000,000)
The Maximum function ensures floor at 0 for all scenarios

evidence.quote: A margin credit (normally 5,000,000) is granted to each CP
Net margin after credit = Maximum [0 , (Net margin – Margin credit)]
